rationale: In the theory section on time framing, the citing paper discusses LeBoeuf (2006) in detail, summarizing its finding that extent-versus-date descriptions change discounting and quoting its conclusion that equivalent time descriptions are not psychologically interchangeable. This evidence is used to motivate the paper's own core premise that wording of a redemption window can alter perceived duration/feasibility and downstream promotion responses even when objective time is unchanged. Because the cited work helps establish the conceptual basis for the citing paper's hypotheses rather than serving as a passing citation, the role is substantive.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on LeBoeuf (2006) as conceptual groundwork showing that equivalent time descriptions can yield different judgments. It uses that premise to build its own framing predictions for promotion redemption windows.
